steps.title: Track the migration pattern
steps.content: The passage links regions of China with first languages: immigrants from north China, where Mandarin is common, tend to settle in Queens, while immigrants from south China, where Cantonese or Fuzhounese is common, tend to settle in Manhattan.

steps.title: Choose the supported inference
steps.content: That pattern shows a regional language relationship from one country appearing again after people emigrate to a new country. Choice A discusses language change, which the passage never mentions. Choice C concerns changes within languages after immigration, and Choice D makes an unsupported claim about country size. Only the regional-language migration pattern is supported. The answer is B.
